tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function with possible tubular involvement. Low total CO₂ may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Hemoglobin A1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria, possibly due to recent onset hyperglycemia or analytical interference. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and diabetes mellitus.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
